methods.description: This method involves the preparation of a copal specimen for radiocarbon dating and subsequent analysis to determine its age.

steps.description: The piece was cut and polished at the SMF using a Phoenix Beta polishing machine with grinding paper for metallography wet and dry: Grip 1200, 2500, and 4000.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut and polish specimen using Phoenix Beta polishing machine

steps.description: After being polished, it was stored following Sadowski's et al. recommendations.
steps.searchable_summary: Store polished specimen according to Sadowski's recommendations

steps.description: The age was determined using rigorous standard methods of radiocarbon analysis (14C and 13C) in the Beta Analytic Inc. Laboratories.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform radiocarbon analysis at Beta Analytic Inc.

methods.description: This method involves the use of synchrotron-radiation based X-ray microtomography to analyze the specimen's internal structure.

steps.description: The specimen was analysed using synchrotron-radiation based X-ray microtomography at the Imaging Beamline-IBL P05-PETRA III at Deutsches Elektronen Synchrotron (DESY) in Hamburg.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze specimen using SRμCT at DESY

steps.description: 3601 projections, equally spaced between 0 and π, were recorded for a tomographic scan at a photon energy of 18 keV.

Tomographic reconstruction was achieved by applying a transport-of-intensity phase retrieval with a beamline-specific workflow implemented in Matlab, using the filtered-back projection algorithm implemented in the Astra Toolbox.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform tomographic reconstruction using Matlab and Astra Toolbox

steps.description: Raw projections have been binned two times for processing, resulting in an effective pixel size of 1.28 µm in the reconstructed tomographic volume.
The specimens were segmented in three dimensions using region-growing techniques in VGStudioMax.

methods.description: This method involves capturing and processing micrographs and Z-stack images of the specimen using a compound microscope.

steps.description: The micrographs and Z-stack images were recorded under a compound microscope Olympus CX41 equipped with a digital camera sCMEX-20.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture micrographs and Z-stack images using Olympus CX41

steps.description: These micrographs were processed with Image Focus Alpha.
steps.searchable_summary: Process micrographs using Image Focus Alpha

methods.description: This method involves creating figures for the paper using graphic design software.

steps.description: Figures were created using CorelDRAW Graphics Suite software.
